automotive-ev-battery.title: UK removes HGV rules for larger electric vans
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The UK government will eliminate regulatory barriers for larger electric vans weighing between 3.5 and 4.25 tonnes starting June 2026, aligning them with diesel and petrol counterparts. The reforms remove HGV testing and tachograph requirements, significantly reducing costs and administrative burdens for fleet operators transitioning to zero-emission vehicles.

automotive-ev-battery.title: €1 Billion To Be Invested In German Electric Truck Charging
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Germany's Ministry of Transport announced €1 billion in investment over four years to develop commercial electric truck charging infrastructure across the country. This substantial funding commitment aims to support the nation's transition to zero-emission heavy-duty transport.

automotive-ev-battery.title: MAN unveils electric 16-tonne truck eTGM
automotive-ev-battery.summary: MAN has unveiled the eTGM, an electric version of its medium-duty TGM distribution truck, which will become available starting next year. The new offering represents a significant expansion of electric options in the commercial vehicle segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Scania demonstrates fully electric concrete transport solution
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Scania, partnering with Unicon and Liebherr, has developed a battery-electric truck with a fully integrated electric concrete mixer system. The Swedish automaker positions this development as a scalable model for deployment rather than merely a pilot project.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD Exported More Vehicles In April Than Tesla Sold Worldwide
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD exported 135,098 vehicles in April 2026, surpassing Tesla's total monthly sales worldwide, all of which were battery electric or plug-in hybrid vehicles. This milestone underscores the Chinese automaker's growing dominance in global EV market share.

automotive-ev-battery.title: RoB@t2Cell project aims to make battery recycling smarter
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The RoB@t2Cell research project, led by Umicore and Fraunhofer IPA, aims to develop innovative methods for safe disassembly and targeted discharge of battery cells for recycling and second-life applications. The project will enable selective discharge of only those cells unsuitable for reuse.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Kia ships first PV5 units to Japan ahead of market launch
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia has begun exporting its PV5 vehicle to Japan, marking final preparations for its spring market launch as the company's first purpose-built vehicle in the country. The shipments signal the South Korean automaker's strategic expansion into the Japanese commercial vehicle market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Blink Charging joins forces with Emobi on electric car charging
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Blink Charging has partnered with Emobi to integrate its fleet and public charging infrastructure into Emobi's unified roaming ecosystem and JustPlug platform. The collaboration aims to provide seamless roaming and automated charging experiences across one of North America's largest EV charging networks.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD puts 500th electric bus on Japanese roads
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese automaker BYD has deployed six additional electric buses in Japan, bringing its total fleet operating on Japanese roads to 500 units. The milestone demonstrates BYD's significant penetration in the Japanese public transportation market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD plans to launch new Yuan Plus overseas as Atto 5
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD has begun planning the overseas launch of its second-generation Yuan Plus, which was unveiled at the Beijing Auto Show in April, to be marketed internationally as the Atto 5. The expansion represents the company's strategy to strengthen its global EV portfolio.

world-news.title: Trade, Iran and Taiwan on the agenda as Trump arrives in China for high-stakes talks with Xi
world-news.summary: US President Trump has arrived in Beijing for high-stakes negotiations with Chinese leader Xi Jinping, marking the first visit by a sitting US president since 2017. The discussions are expected to center on trade relations, Iran policy, and the contested status of Taiwan, with significant implications for global economic and geopolitical stability.

world-news.title: Trump-Xi summit live: US, China leaders to hold talks on trade, tech, Iran
world-news.summary: The Trump-Xi summit represents a critical moment for the global economy as the two superpowers engage in negotiations on trade, technology policy, and Iran-related matters. The talks come amid ongoing economic tensions between the United States and China and regional instability affecting international commerce.

world-news.title: Deadly Russian drone attacks on Ukraine resume after ceasefire expires
world-news.summary: Russian drone attacks on Ukraine have resumed following the expiration of a ceasefire agreement, resulting in at least six confirmed deaths. Ukrainian President Zelensky has warned of additional waves of strikes expected to continue through Wednesday, escalating the ongoing conflict.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Tehran slams 'collusion' as Netanyahu 'secretly' visits UAE
world-news.summary: Iran has accused regional partners of collusion after reports of a covert visit by Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu to the United Arab Emirates. Tehran has also reiterated its claims regarding control of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil trade.

world-news.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 22 people, health ministry says
world-news.summary: Israeli air strikes on southern Lebanon have resulted in at least 22 deaths, including eight children, according to the Lebanese health ministry. The strikes represent a significant escalation in regional tensions and ongoing military operations in the area.

world-news.title: Trump administration offers $100m in aid to Cuba in exchange for reform
world-news.summary: The Trump administration has proposed a $100 million aid package to Cuba contingent upon political and economic reforms, citing the island nation's communist leadership as an impediment to humanitarian assistance. The offer comes amid ongoing economic restrictions and oil blockades affecting Cuba.

world-news.title: Trump-Xi summit: Why Beijing might have the advantage
world-news.summary: Economic analysts suggest that China may hold negotiating advantages in the Trump-Xi summit, as Chinese exports have reached record highs despite trade tensions since Trump's return to office. The disparity reflects broader shifts in global trade patterns that could influence the outcomes of bilateral discussions.

world-news.title: Gunshots fired in Philippine Senate where lawmaker wanted by ICC is holed up
world-news.summary: Armed confrontation erupted at the Philippine Senate as authorities attempted to apprehend Senator Ronald dela Rosa, a former police chief indicted by the International Criminal Court for his role in enforcing a deadly drug war campaign. The incident raises questions about judicial sovereignty and international criminal accountability.

world-news.title: Philip Davis and Progressive Liberal Party win general election in Bahamas
world-news.summary: The Progressive Liberal Party has won the Bahamas general election, marking the first time in three decades that a single political party has secured consecutive electoral victories in the Caribbean nation. The result has implications for regional governance and policy continuity in the Caribbean.

world-news.title: Trump taps David Venturella, former private prison executive, to lead ICE
world-news.summary: The Trump administration has appointed David Venturella, a former executive with the GEO Group private prison corporation, to lead Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The appointment signals a significant shift in US immigration enforcement policy with potential implications for border management and detention practices.

finance-markets.title: Senate confirms Warsh to succeed Powell as Fed chair
finance-markets.summary: The Senate has confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next Chair of the Federal Reserve, concluding a contentious selection process. This leadership transition marks a significant shift in US monetary policy direction and has major implications for interest rates and economic management.

finance-markets.title: US sells 30-year bonds at 5% yield for first time since 2007
finance-markets.summary: The US Treasury auctioned $25 billion in 30-year bonds at a 5% yield, marking the highest level since 2007 and reflecting mounting inflation pressures. The sale occurred as fresh data revealed the sharpest producer price increases since Russia's invasion of Ukraine, signaling persistent inflationary headwinds.

finance-markets.title: What's At Stake as Xi and Trump Meet in Beijing
finance-markets.summary: President Trump is conducting a significant state visit to China, the first by a US leader in nine years, to address critical issues between the world's two largest economies including trade, technology, and geopolitical tensions. The summit carries substantial implications for global markets and financial stability amid ongoing regional conflicts.

finance-markets.title: War Turmoil Saps Asian Reserves With Philippines, India Hit Most
finance-markets.summary: Foreign-exchange reserves across Asia are declining sharply as central banks deplete funds to defend their currencies against currency pressure stemming from elevated oil prices caused by regional conflict. The Philippines and India are experiencing the most significant impacts on their reserve positions.

finance-markets.title: Trump demands Xi 'open' China to US business
finance-markets.summary: During his Beijing summit, President Trump has called for greater market access for American businesses in China, amid ongoing trade and technology disputes. The visit occurs under significant geopolitical tension and underscores the contentious bilateral economic relationship.

finance-markets.title: Blackstone REIT Raises $1.75 Billion in IPO to Buy Data Centers
finance-markets.summary: Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust raised $1.75 billion in a US initial public offering to acquire data center assets, demonstrating continued robust investor appetite for artificial intelligence infrastructure investments. The offering reflects strong market demand for tech-enabled infrastructure assets.

finance-markets.title: Oil Steadies Ahead of Trump-Xi Talks as Iran War Tensions Simmer
finance-markets.summary: Oil prices have stabilized as markets await the outcome of Trump-Xi negotiations, though underlying tensions related to the Iran conflict continue to create upside price pressure. The geopolitical backdrop remains a key driver of commodity market volatility.

finance-markets.title: Citadel tells key researchers to relocate from Hong Kong or quit
finance-markets.summary: Citadel has issued an ultimatum to members of its quantitative strategies team based in Hong Kong, requiring relocation or resignation from the hedge fund. The move reflects broader geopolitical concerns and operational reconfiguration among major financial institutions in the region.

finance-markets.title: Chip IPO Highlights Malaysia Deals Surging Toward 13-Year High
finance-markets.summary: Malaysia's IPO market is experiencing a significant surge with a wave of upcoming listings including a chip design firm, positioning the market toward its highest annual volume in 13 years. The activity reflects growing investor interest in Asia-Pacific semiconductor and technology ventures.

finance-markets.title: India Bans Sugar Exports Through September to Protect Supply
finance-markets.summary: India, the world's second-largest sugar producer, has implemented an export ban through September to safeguard domestic supply availability. The protectionist measure reflects government efforts to manage domestic commodity availability and prices amid global supply uncertainties.

science-space.title: NASA Outlines Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plans
science-space.summary: NASA is advancing plans for the Artemis III crewed mission in Earth orbit, which will test rendezvous and docking capabilities between the Orion spacecraft and commercial lunar landers from Blue Origin and SpaceX. This mission represents a critical step toward NASA's goal of conducting crewed landings at the Moon's South Pole region.

science-space.title: 'Like putting a microscope into the core of the sun': World's 1st space-based neutrino detector launches to orbit
science-space.summary: The world's first space-based neutrino detector has launched to orbit this month to study elusive neutrino particles that constantly bombard Earth. This breakthrough instrument will provide unprecedented insights into fundamental physics and solar processes.

science-space.title: Studying Pneumonia in Space for Heart Health on Earth
science-space.summary: Expedition 74 astronauts aboard the International Space Station are investigating how bacteria that cause pneumonia can lead to long-term heart damage using stem cell-derived heart tissues in microgravity. This research leverages the unique space environment to develop new methods for managing cardiovascular health and infectious diseases on Earth.

science-space.title: Blue Origin's lunar lander mockup is ready for NASA Artemis astronaut training
science-space.summary: A mockup of Blue Origin's Blue Moon Mark 2 lunar lander has been assembled at NASA's Johnson Space Center and is prepared for Artemis astronauts to begin hands-on training. This training facility represents a major milestone in preparing astronauts for future crewed lunar missions.

science-space.title: Will Starship launch from foreign shores? SpaceX 'constantly exploring' options for megarocket liftoff sites
science-space.summary: SpaceX has revealed it is actively exploring additional launch sites for its Starship megarocket, considering locations both within and outside the United States. This expansion strategy reflects the company's ambitions to increase launch frequency and operational flexibility for its heavy-lift vehicle.

science-space.title: What flings mysteriously powerful particles called 'cosmic rays' at Earth?
science-space.summary: Scientists are investigating the sources of ultra-high-energy cosmic rays that are 10 million times more powerful than particles produced in Earth's strongest particle accelerators. Breakthrough research may finally solve a 60-year-old mystery regarding what generates these mysterious high-energy particles.

science-space.title: 30-mile-high clouds of acid on Venus are made by the largest 'hydraulic jump' in the solar system
science-space.summary: Scientists have discovered that Venus's massive 3,700-mile-long sulfuric acid cloud bank forms through a hydraulic jump phenomenon similar to water spreading in a kitchen sink. This finding provides new insights into the extreme atmospheric dynamics of Earth's neighboring planet.

science-space.title: NASA-Supported Space Tech Advances Earthly Construction
science-space.summary: Technology developed to support NASA's lunar habitat construction is now being applied to advance building on Earth through an innovative Freeform 3D Printing process. Branch Technology Inc. has developed the technique to create structures with lightweight lattice frameworks that are both efficient and structurally strong.

science-space.title: Lasers shine a new light on the space junk air pollution problem
science-space.summary: Scientists have developed a new laser-based technique to study incoming space debris and analyze its effects on Earth's atmosphere. This research addresses the growing challenge of space junk pollution and its environmental impact.

science-space.title: NASA's Planet-Hunting TESS Reveals Dazzling Night Sky
science-space.summary: NASA's TESS mission has released its most comprehensive view of the night sky to date, advancing the search for exoplanets and expanding our understanding of the cosmos. This comprehensive data release represents a major milestone in the mission's ongoing discovery objectives.

tech-ai.title: Notion just turned its workspace into a hub for AI agents
tech-ai.summary: Notion has launched a new developer platform enabling teams to integrate AI agents, external data sources, and custom code directly into their workspace. The move represents a significant expansion of Notion's capabilities in agentic productivity software, allowing organizations to automate and streamline workflows with AI-powered tools.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic's Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic's head of product for Claude Code outlines a vision for the next evolution of artificial intelligence centered on proactive systems. Wu suggests that advanced AI will shift from reactive assistance to predictively understanding and addressing user needs before they are explicitly articulated.

tech-ai.title: Geothermal startup Fervo Energy pops 33% in IPO debut fueled by AI data center demand
tech-ai.summary: Enhanced geothermal company Fervo Energy saw its initial public offering surge 33 percent on its first trading day, with underwriters expanding the offering multiple times due to robust investor interest. The strong reception reflects surging demand for sustainable power solutions to fuel the computational requirements of artificial intelligence data centers.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft's Edge Copilot update uses AI to pull information from across your tabs
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft Edge is introducing a new Copilot feature that allows users to ask their AI assistant questions about content across all open browser tabs, enabling comparisons, summaries, and cross-tab information gathering. The update enhances productivity by letting users selectively enable or disable specific Copilot experiences while retiring the previous Copilot Mode.

tech-ai.title: Mark Zuckerberg announces 'completely private' encrypted Meta AI chat
tech-ai.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg unveiled Incognito Chat, a new AI conversation feature that employs end-to-end encryption and stores no conversation logs on company servers. The offering differentiates itself from competing incognito AI modes by combining encryption technology with the absence of server-side data retention, addressing privacy concerns around AI interactions.

tech-ai.title: Origin Lab raises $8M to help video game companies sell data to world-model builders
tech-ai.summary: Origin Lab has secured $8 million in funding to create a marketplace connecting AI laboratories with high-quality licensed data from video game companies. The platform addresses growing demand for specialized training data as AI developers build advanced world models and simulation systems.

tech-ai.title: AMD's best CPU tech for gamers is coming to workstations too
tech-ai.summary: AMD is expanding its high-performance 3D V-Cache technology to commercial workstation processors with its refreshed Ryzen PRO 9000 series lineup. The move extends AMD's gaming-focused architecture innovations to professional creative and computational workloads, where testing has demonstrated significant performance improvements.

tech-ai.title: Musk's xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
tech-ai.summary: xAI's Colossus 2 data center in Mississippi is facing legal challenges over its operation of approximately 50 gas turbines as power plants without apparent regulatory oversight. The lawsuit raises environmental and compliance concerns regarding the company's approach to powering its AI computational infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: Rivian spinoff Mind Robotics raises another $400M
tech-ai.summary: Mind Robotics, the robotics company spun out from electric vehicle manufacturer Rivian, has secured an additional $400 million in funding, bringing its total raised to over $1 billion. The substantial capital infusion underscores investor confidence in the robotics sector's growth potential.

tech-ai.title: Netflix's ad ambitions just keep growing
tech-ai.summary: Netflix's advertising-supported tier more than doubled its user base for the second consecutive year, reaching over 250 million monthly viewers globally and generating $1.5 billion in ad revenue during 2025. The streaming giant plans to expand ad placement opportunities throughout its service in the coming year as it continues monetizing its platform.

crypto-web3.title: Clarity Act amendments would remake key parts of crypto bill but have doubtful future
crypto-web3.summary: The U.S. crypto market structure bill faces dozens of amendments ahead of its Senate Banking Committee hearing, with proposed changes targeting stablecoins, DeFi, and ethics provisions. The amendments signal ongoing legislative debate over how to regulate digital assets, though their passage remains uncertain.

crypto-web3.title: More than 100 amendments filed targeting stablecoins, ethics and DeFi ahead of Senate Banking Committee Clarity Act vote
crypto-web3.summary: Over 100 amendments have been filed by Senate Banking Committee members targeting the Clarity Act, focusing on stablecoins, ethics, and DeFi regulation. The committee is scheduled to hold a markup and vote on the legislation on Thursday.

crypto-web3.title: UK parliament to probe Nigel Farage's $6.8 million donation from crypto billionaire
crypto-web3.summary: The UK Parliament is investigating Reform UK party leader Nigel Farage's $6.8 million donation from a crypto billionaire for potential violations of parliamentary conduct rules. If found guilty, Farage could face suspension from the House of Commons.

crypto-web3.title: Ethereum app builder Consensys has delayed its potential IPO until fall
crypto-web3.summary: MetaMask wallet developer Consensys has postponed its anticipated initial public offering to the fall, moving away from earlier timelines. The company had engaged JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs to lead the IPO process.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto wallet provider Ledger puts U.S. IPO plans on hold due to market conditions
crypto-web3.summary: French hardware wallet manufacturer Ledger has suspended its plans for a U.S. IPO, which had been valued at approximately $4 billion, citing volatile market conditions and weakened investor appetite for crypto sector public offerings. The company is reassessing its timeline for going public.

crypto-web3.title: Animoca-backed NUVA connects Figure's $19 billion of tokenized assets to Ethereum
crypto-web3.summary: The NUVA protocol, led by veteran BNY executive Anthony Moro and backed by Animoca, has bridged Figure's $19 billion in tokenized real-world assets to Ethereum's DeFi ecosystem. The initiative initially focuses on home equity lines of credit and Treasury securities to connect traditional finance with decentralized markets.

crypto-web3.title: Telecom giant KDDI to acquire 14.9% stake in Coincheck Group in $65 million deal
crypto-web3.summary: Japanese telecommunications major KDDI has agreed to acquire a 14.9% stake in Coincheck Group for $65 million while establishing a business alliance for revenue sharing and customer referrals. The partnership aims to expand cryptocurrency access and services across KDDI's customer base in Japan.

crypto-web3.title: Fidelity International's first tokenized fund receives top-tier AAA-mf rating from Moody's
crypto-web3.summary: Fidelity International's tokenized fund FILQ has received a top-tier AAA-mf rating from Moody's, enabling 24/7 redemptions and settlements. The fund is modeled on Fidelity's existing low-volatility net asset value fund and represents institutional-grade adoption of tokenized finance.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin buyers with long-term 'conviction' surges 300% with most recent buyers sitting on profits
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin holdings by long-term conviction buyers has surged to approximately 4 million BTC, representing a roughly 300% increase since late 2025, indicating strong accumulation by strategic investors. Most recent buyers are currently profitable, suggesting growing confidence in Bitcoin's value proposition.

crypto-web3.title: Jupiter taps Bitwise to curate institutional-grade Solana-based USDe lending market
crypto-web3.summary: Jupiter Lend has partnered with Bitwise and Fluid to establish an institutional-grade lending market for the USDe synthetic stablecoin on Solana. The offering provides a regulated, curated avenue for investors to earn yield on digital assets.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released patches for 167 security vulnerabilities including a SharePoint Server zero-day and the publicly disclosed Windows Defender flaw "BlueHammer." Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and Adobe Reader addressed an actively exploited remote code execution vulnerability.

cybersecurity.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
cybersecurity.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited known vulnerabilities in older internet routers to mass harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks without deploying malicious code. The sophisticated campaign allowed state-backed actors to gain unauthorized access to corporate systems at scale.

cybersecurity.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
cybersecurity.summary: An Iran-linked hacktivist group claimed responsibility for a destructive data-wiping attack against medical device manufacturer Stryker, forcing the company to send home over 5,000 workers at its Ireland facility. The company reported building emergencies and operational disruptions across multiple locations.

cybersecurity.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: U.S. and international authorities dismantled four major IoT botnets—Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad—that compromised over three million Internet of Things devices including routers and cameras. The botnets were responsible for record-breaking distributed denial-of-service attacks capable of disrupting critical online services.

cybersecurity.title: 'Scattered Spider' Member 'Tylerb' Pleads Guilty
cybersecurity.summary: A 24-year-old British cybercriminal and senior member of the "Scattered Spider" group pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and identity theft for his role in text-message phishing attacks against major technology companies in summer 2022. The attacks compromised at least a dozen firms and resulted in theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

cybersecurity.title: Germany Doxes "UNKN," Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
cybersecurity.summary: German authorities identified and publicly exposed "UNKN," a 31-year-old Russian named Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin who led the ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil. Shchukin orchestrated at least 130 computer sabotage and extortion attacks against victims across Germany between 2019 and 2021.

cybersecurity.title: Azerbaijani Energy Firm Hit by Repeated Microsoft Exchange Exploitation
cybersecurity.summary: A China-affiliated threat actor attributed to the FamousSparrow group conducted a multi-wave intrusion campaign against an Azerbaijani oil and gas company from December 2025 through February 2026, exploiting Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities. The campaign represents an expansion of the group's targeting beyond hospitality, telecom, and government sectors.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released security patches for 138 vulnerabilities across its product portfolio, with 30 rated Critical and 104 rated Important, though none were reported as publicly known or under active attack. The update includes 61 privilege escalation bugs affecting DNS and Netlogon services.

cybersecurity.title: GemStuffer Abuses 150+ RubyGems to Exfiltrate Scraped U.K. Council Portal Data
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers identified a campaign called GemStuffer that uploaded over 150 malicious packages to the RubyGems repository for data exfiltration rather than malware distribution. The packages contained scrapers targeting UK government portal data and leveraged the registry as a covert command-and-control channel.

cybersecurity.title: Android Adds Intrusion Logging for Sophisticated Spyware Forensics
cybersecurity.summary: Google introduced Intrusion Logging, an opt-in Android feature available in Advanced Protection Mode that enables persistent forensic logging for analyzing sophisticated spyware attacks. The privacy-preserving capability allows security investigators to analyze suspected compromises and improve threat detection.

health-biotech.title: U.S. overdose deaths fell again in 2025, but some worry about policy and drug supply changes
health-biotech.summary: Preliminary government data shows approximately 70,000 Americans died from drug overdoses in 2025, representing a 14% decline from the previous year. Despite the encouraging trend, public health experts express concerns about potential impacts from policy shifts and changes to the drug supply landscape.

health-biotech.title: Tessera reveals strongest data yet for in vivo sickle cell therapy
health-biotech.summary: Tessera Therapeutics has announced compelling data for an experimental gene editing therapy for sickle cell disease that can be administered as a simple infusion without requiring complex cell engineering or chemotherapy preconditioning. The approach positions the therapy as a competitive alternative to existing commercial cell therapies.

health-biotech.title: Lilly points to low-dose Zepbound and Foundayo as weight loss maintenance options
health-biotech.summary: New trial data indicates that a reduced dose of Eli Lilly's obesity medication Zepbound may offer a safe and effective option for weight loss maintenance at potentially lower cost. The findings suggest an opportunity to optimize dosing strategies for long-term patient management of obesity.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: What the Trump administration wants in its next FDA leader
health-biotech.summary: Trump administration officials have outlined priorities for the next FDA commissioner, including rebuilding public trust, implementing food policy reforms, and accelerating drug approval processes. The leadership transition marks a significant shift in regulatory direction for the pharmaceutical and medical device industries.

health-biotech.title: EU reaches provisional deal on Critical Medicines Act
health-biotech.summary: The European Parliament and Council have provisionally approved the Critical Medicines Act, a regulatory framework designed to strengthen and secure supply chains for essential medications across the European Union. The legislation represents a coordinated policy response to pharmaceutical supply vulnerabilities.

health-biotech.title: After an apparent cure, a gene editing company struggles to repeat the magic
health-biotech.summary: iECURE's experimental gene therapy initially appeared to cure a baby of a life-threatening genetic disease, but subsequent results in a larger patient cohort have raised questions about reproducibility and efficacy. The findings underscore ongoing challenges in translating early successes in gene therapy to consistent clinical outcomes.

health-biotech.title: New subpoena suggests DOJ has begun criminal investigation of gender-affirming care
health-biotech.summary: Federal grand jury subpoenas issued to multiple hospitals indicate the Department of Justice has initiated a criminal investigation into gender-affirming care provided to minors. The development reflects intensifying regulatory scrutiny of this contentious medical practice area.

health-biotech.title: The 'velocity of obesity' aims to show which nations are slowing an epidemic
health-biotech.summary: A new study analyzing obesity trends reveals that U.S. childhood obesity rates have plateaued while adult obesity continues to rise, with American rates remaining substantially higher than peer nations. The research introduces the concept of obesity 'velocity' to better track epidemic progression across populations.

health-biotech.title: Takeda to target 4,500 jobs as incoming CEO continues restructuring
health-biotech.summary: Japanese pharmaceutical company Takeda announced plans to eliminate approximately 4,500 positions, representing roughly 9% of its workforce, as the incoming CEO advances a multiyear restructuring initiative. The layoffs reflect broader industry consolidation and operational optimization trends.

health-biotech.title: Abeona pivots to T cells; AstraZeneca's rare disease drug passes Phase 3
health-biotech.summary: Biotech company Abeona is shifting its strategic focus from eye gene therapies to solid tumor T cell therapy development, while AstraZeneca's investigational rare disease drug has achieved positive Phase 3 trial results. The moves reflect evolving therapeutic priorities within the gene therapy and cell therapy sectors.

startups-vc.title: Anduril raises $5B, doubles valuation to $61B
startups-vc.summary: Defense tech startup Anduril Industries has secured $5 billion in funding at a $61 billion valuation, doubling its previous valuation following $2.2 billion in revenue in 2025. The round was led by Thrive and Andreessen Horowitz, underscoring record venture capital flowing into the defense technology sector.

startups-vc.title: Cerebras Sees Sizzling Demand For IPO Shares
startups-vc.summary: AI chip startup Cerebras Systems is making its long-awaited public market debut on Nasdaq after years of private fundraising and previously abandoned IPO plans. The company's initial public offering reflects strong investor appetite for AI infrastructure companies.

startups-vc.title: Fazeshift Scores $17M As Investors Bet On AI-Powered Finance Ops, Starting With Accounts Receivable
startups-vc.summary: Fazeshift, an AI-powered automation platform for accounts receivable operations, has raised $17 million in Series A funding. The investment demonstrates growing venture capital confidence in AI agents designed to streamline enterprise finance operations.

startups-vc.title: From Credit Cards To An AI Concierge: How Amex Ventures Backs Startups Building Autonomous Commerce
startups-vc.summary: Amex Ventures is investing in startups building autonomous commerce platforms and AI-powered agents that function as intelligent concierge services. The corporate venture arm's thesis reflects a broader industry shift toward agentic AI applications in consumer and commercial contexts.

startups-vc.title: Exclusive: Physician-Founded Saile Raises $2.2M To Help Doctors Find Side Jobs Using AI
startups-vc.summary: Saile, a physician-founded startup, has raised $2.2 million in pre-seed funding to build an AI-powered credentialing and staffing platform connecting doctors with side gig opportunities. The platform addresses workforce flexibility and income diversification trends in healthcare.

startups-vc.title: Announcing the YC AI Stack
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has partnered with nearly two dozen companies to provide over $25,000 in free AI development tool credits to YC event attendees. The initiative aims to democratize access to AI infrastructure and accelerate startup development across the YC network.

startups-vc.title: Voice AI in India is hard — Wispr Flow is betting on it anyway
startups-vc.summary: Wispr Flow reports accelerated growth in India following its Hinglish language rollout, despite ongoing challenges in the voice AI sector. The startup's expansion into regional Indian languages demonstrates increasing demand for localized AI applications in emerging markets.

startups-vc.title: Sector Snapshot: Agtech Startups Face A Drier Funding Climate
startups-vc.summary: Venture funding to agriculture-related startups is on track to match or decline slightly versus recent years, with deal count expected to fall more sharply despite AI-driven companies in the space. The funding slowdown reflects tepid investor enthusiasm for agtech in the post-pandemic period.

startups-vc.title: Adding Canada Back to Our List of Accepted Countries of Incorporation
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has reinstated Canada as an approved country of incorporation in response to feedback from its Canadian founder community. The move expands opportunities for Canadian entrepreneurs to participate in the Y Combinator program.

startups-vc.title: Introducing the 6 stages at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 — built for today's tougher startup market
startups-vc.summary: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature 200+ sessions across six stages from October 13-15, led by 250+ industry leaders. Early-bird registration offers up to $410 in savings plus a 50% discount on additional passes.

energy-climate.title: Santa Marta: Key outcomes from first summit on 'transitioning away' from fossil fuels
energy-climate.summary: Countries at a historic first-of-its-kind summit have committed to developing national roadmaps for transitioning away from fossil fuels, marking a significant diplomatic milestone in global climate policy. This represents the first international consensus on concrete plans to phase down fossil fuel dependence.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: Wind and solar have saved UK from gas imports worth £1.7bn since Iran war began
energy-climate.summary: Renewable energy generation in the UK has prevented the need for costly gas imports valued at £1.7 billion since geopolitical tensions began, demonstrating the economic resilience benefits of renewable deployment. The analysis underscores how clean energy reduces vulnerability to international energy supply disruptions.

energy-climate.title: China Briefing 30 April 2026: Fossil fuel 'strict controls' | El Niño approaches | Why cleantech exports have surged
energy-climate.summary: China is implementing stricter controls on fossil fuel development while experiencing a surge in cleantech exports, positioning itself as a global renewable energy leader despite approaching El Niño conditions. The briefing highlights China's dual strategy of domestic decarbonization and international green technology dominance.

energy-climate.title: Large-scale PV could intensify water stress in China's Tarim Basin
energy-climate.summary: Chinese researchers warn that massive photovoltaic deployment in the Taklamakan Desert may alter regional climate dynamics and exacerbate water scarcity in the already arid Tarim Basin, despite the region's substantial solar potential. The findings highlight potential environmental trade-offs between renewable energy expansion and water resource management.

energy-climate.title: Solar capture factors fall across Europe as negative price hours surge in key markets
energy-climate.summary: European solar markets are experiencing declining capture factors due to strong renewable output and weak demand, with France, Germany, Italy, Poland, and Spain seeing increased periods of negative electricity pricing. The trend reflects market pressures as solar penetration rises across the continent.

energy-climate.title: Alsym and Juniper to deploy 500 MWh of sodium-ion battery storage
energy-climate.summary: California-based renewables developer Juniper has agreed to deploy 500 MWh of sodium-ion battery storage from Alsym Energy, building on the company's earlier 8.5 GWh partnership with ESS iron flow batteries. The agreement accelerates adoption of alternative battery chemistries for grid-scale energy storage.

energy-climate.title: Continuous-wave infrared laser technology enables damage-free backsheet removal in end-of-life solar modules
energy-climate.summary: U.S. researchers have developed an innovative IR laser method to remove backsheets from end-of-life solar panels without damaging glass or wafers, offering a lower-cost and lower-energy recycling alternative. The breakthrough technology supports circular economy goals in the solar industry.

energy-climate.title: Ørsted and PGE start offshore works on Baltica 2 wind farm
energy-climate.summary: Ørsted and Polish energy company PGE have begun offshore construction on the Baltica 2 wind farm in the Baltic Sea, advancing Eastern Europe's renewable energy infrastructure. The project represents significant progress in expanding wind capacity in the region.

energy-climate.title: South Africa's Eskom resorts to grid-scale gravity energy storage
energy-climate.summary: South Africa's state-owned utility Eskom is deploying grid-scale gravity energy storage technology to address power supply challenges. The initiative demonstrates innovative approaches to energy storage beyond conventional battery solutions.

energy-climate.title: Rooftop heat pump tech with metal-organic framework dehumidification tested by Amazon
energy-climate.summary: Amazon has completed a six-month field trial of an advanced rooftop heat pump system from startup Transaera featuring metal-organic framework dehumidification technology at a logistics facility. The trial tests innovative approaches to improving building energy efficiency at scale.

asia-pacific.title: Xi and Trump to sit down for far-reaching talks on China-US ties and global shocks
asia-pacific.summary:
This live article is freely available to our registered users. Please log in or create an account below.
Unrivalled Xi-Trump summit analysis: get real-time updates and exclusive boots-on-the-ground reporting from our Beijing and Washington bureaus. Subscribe now with great savings to stay ahead.
US President Donald Trump will meet Chinese President Xi Jinping today for a much-anticipated summit that could shape the next stage of rivalry between the world’s two superpowers.
It is the first visit.

asia-pacific.title: A year after USAID cuts, Philippine development groups struggle as anger lingers
asia-pacific.summary:
A year after Washington froze and then slashed foreign aid, development workers in the Philippines and across the region say the damage is still being felt, with job losses and abandoned projects continuing to threaten some of Asia’s most vulnerable groups.
“On a personal level, it was the loss of income. But we lost a lot of critical projects in the country,” Sharon*, a former country representative for a US-based organisation overseeing these projects in the Philippines, said in an...

asia-pacific.title: Centuries ahead of Wall Street: how China’s Song dynasty pioneered global fiat currency
asia-pacific.summary:
At least gold glitters. Long before the first paper banknotes fluttered through the financial houses of Europe, the bustling markets of 11th century China birthed an unprecedented financial “innovation”: the use of paper currency.
In the 1020s, burdened by the shortage of bronze coins and a rapidly expanding economy, merchants in the southwestern province of Sichuan began issuing paper promissory notes. This marked the genesis of jiaozi, the world’s earliest paper money. Soon, with the imperial.

asia-pacific.title: Jimmy Lai case shows press freedom is not a get-out-of-jail-free ticket
asia-pacific.summary:
The death of Ted Turner, who founded CNN in 1980 as the world’s first 24-hour television news channel, is a poignant reminder of the glory days of the fourth estate. CNN made day-and-night news coverage, including at the front line of battlefields, an everyday experience for many people.
The rise of the internet and ever more channels of news delivery has enabled a global audience of news consumers, attuned to reports delivered from all around the world. This has turned the most successful news.

asia-pacific.title: Who is Ronald dela Rosa, the ICC-wanted lawmaker holed up in the Philippine Senate?
asia-pacific.summary:
Philippine Senator Ronald dela Rosa called on the public to step in to prevent law enforcement agents ⁠from handing him over to the ⁠International Criminal Court as the sound of gunfire rattled through ⁠the Senate building on Wednesday evening.
Dela Rosa stands accused by the ICC of crimes against humanity related to the war on drugs he oversaw while serving as police chief under then-president Rodrigo Duterte. Dela Rosa, 64, has denied involvement in illegal killings.
Duterte, in office from...

asia-pacific.title: ‘Basically a Chinese racket’: how China streaked ahead at a global AI conference
asia-pacific.summary:
Chinese universities have significantly overtaken their American rivals in research output at one of the world’s leading artificial intelligence (AI) conferences, according to an analysis of more than 5,000 accepted papers that went viral on social media.
Among the top 50 institutions contributing to the International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR), held in Rio de Janeiro last month, mainland China accounted for about 44 per cent of the total, with Tsinghua University, Shanghai...

asia-pacific.title: China parent brings son, 12, to work, where he plays with fire, destroying entire office
asia-pacific.summary:
A 12-year-old boy in China who was taken to work by one of his parents literally played with fire and left an office completely destroyed.
The parent, whose gender is unknown, took the boy to an office in Ningbo in the eastern province of Zhejiang on April 30 in an effort to enhance their emotional bond, the Red Star News reported.
It is not clear what kind of company this parent works for.

While busy with work, the parent left the boy alone in the office. The youngster became bored and came up

asia-pacific.title: US’ Rubio calls on China to play an ‘active’ role in ending Iran war, despite Trump rhetoric
asia-pacific.summary:
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Washington hopes to convince Beijing to play a “more active role” in resolving the Iran crisis, a clear sign the issue will come into sharp focus when President Donald Trump holds his closely watched summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
Speaking to Fox News aboard Air Force One, Rubio said the Iran war was a “huge source of instability” and “threatens to destabilise Asia more than any other part of the world because it’s heavily reliant on the straits for

asia-pacific.title: Japan-South Korea ‘comfort women’ row stoked by statues abroad
asia-pacific.summary:
Statues erected by South Korean civic groups on the other side of the world honouring the tens of thousands of women forced into sexual slavery by imperial Japanese forces during World War II have once again succeeded in making Tokyo’s elites deeply uncomfortable.
The decisions by local governments in Germany’s capital and New Zealand’s largest city to rescind or decline to renew permits for “comfort women” memorials have been pounced on by conservatives within Japan’s ruling Liberal Democratic.

asia-pacific.title: Chinese memory module makers ramp up production as CXMT DDR5 breakthrough hits market
asia-pacific.summary:
Chinese memory module manufacturers are accelerating the release of consumer and enterprise storage products powered by domestic DDR5 chips, as breakthroughs by ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT), the nation’s leading memory chipmaker, filter through the supply chain.
Powev, one of China’s major memory module vendors, recently said its Sinker-branded DDR5 server memories had entered mass production and delivery. The 64GB DDR5-5600 RDIMM product passed testing by multiple major customers and was
